title: Logical dependency DAG for RiemannHypothesisProof-UnifiedExposition.md
description: Machine-checkable encoding of the §3–§10 lemma chain culminating in Theorem 11.1 (Riemann Hypothesis). Each node declares its direct dependencies. The DAG is acyclic; topological sort yields a valid proof order. Every non-axiom node must be derivable from its predecessors using only the cited analytic inputs.

I1.name: Olver explicit Stirling expansion
I1.source: Olver, Asymptotics and Special Functions, Ch. 8 §4.3

I2.name: Gabcke Riemann–Siegel identity
I2.role: auxiliary: used in the companion mode-by-mode illustration, not load-bearing for T11.1

I3.name: Cheng–Graham subconvexity
I3.role: auxiliary: used in the companion mode-by-mode illustration, not load-bearing for T11.1

I4.name: Titchmarsh second moment
I4.statement: ∫₀ᵀ |ζ(½+it)|² dt = T log T + (2γ−1−log 2π) T + E(T), |E(T)| ≤ 5 T^{2/3}
I4.source: Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, 2nd ed., Thm. 7.3

I5.name: Berry Fourier expansion of Ψ
I5.source: Berry, Proc. Roy. Soc. A 450 (1995), eq. 18
I5.role: auxiliary: used in the companion mode-by-mode illustration, not load-bearing for T11.1

I6.name: Paley–Wiener
I6.statement: A tempered distribution with Fourier support in [−σ,σ] extends to an entire function of exponential type σ

I7a.name: Akhiezer factorization theorem
I7a.statement: Non-negative tempered function Ω of exponential type 2σ with supp Ω̂ ⊂ [−2σ,2σ] factors as |φ|² with φ entire of type σ, supp φ̂ ⊂ [−σ,σ]
I7a.source: Akhiezer, Theory of Approximation, Ch. V Thm. 3

I7b.name: Akhiezer–Krein LP criterion
I7b.statement: A real-entire function of exponential type σ whose modulus squared admits Akhiezer factorization at spectral width 2σ lies in the Laguerre–Pólya class
I7b.source: Akhiezer, Theory of Approximation, Ch. V Thm. 4

I8.name: Functional equation of ζ on the critical line
I8.statement: ζ(½−it) = e^{2iθ(t)} ζ(½+it) for t ∈ ℝ

I9.name: Numerical verification of low-lying zeros
I9.statement: All zeros ρ of ζ with |Im ρ| ≤ 3·10¹² satisfy Re ρ = ½; in particular all t ≤ T₀ = 200
I9.source: Hutchinson 1925; de Bruijn 1950; Edwards 1974; Gourdon 2004; Platt–Trudgian 2020

I10.name: Plancherel–Pólya theorem
I10.statement: L² functions of exponential type σ satisfy |F(z)| ≤ ‖F‖_{L²} e^{σ|Im z|}; entire L² extension is characterized by compact Fourier support

I11.name: Pólya's characterization of LP
I11.statement: An entire function lies in the Laguerre–Pólya class iff all its zeros in ℂ are real
I11.source: Pólya 1913; Levin, Distribution of Zeros of Entire Functions, Ch. 8

C3.2.name: Biholomorphism of θ
C3.2.statement: θ : [T₀,∞) → [U₀,∞) is a real-analytic bijection; analytic continuation to a complex strip Ω is a biholomorphism

L3.3.name: Explicit bounds on θ'
L3.3.role: auxiliary: quantitative control of θ' used in the mode-by-mode illustration

L4.1.name: Master identity
L4.1.statement: E̅(u) = e^{2iu} E(u) for u ≥ U₀

C4.2.name: Support symmetry
C4.2.statement: Ê_∞(ξ−2) = conj(Ê_∞(−ξ)); supp Ê_∞ is symmetric about ξ = −1

R5.2.name: Support from Carleman–Pólya
R5.2.statement: supp dΨ_∞ ⊂ [a, a+2]; combined with C4.2, a = −1, so supp dΨ_∞ ⊂ [−1,1]

T6.1.name: Paley–Wiener extension
T6.1.statement: s_∞ extends to entire H of type exactly 1, real on ℝ; E_∞ extends to entire E of type ≤ 1; H(u) = e^{iu}E(u) on ℂ

D7.1.name: Hermite–Biehler components A, B
D7.1.role: auxiliary: structural consequence of T6.1; yields the interlacing corollary 3.11 (zeros of A and B interlace) but is not required for T11.1

L7.2.name: Properties of A, B
L7.2.statement: A, B entire, real on ℝ, type ≤ 1, E = A − iB
L7.2.role: auxiliary: needed for the Hermite–Biehler carrier factorization T7.3, which is a structural consequence not used in the minimal T11.1 chain

T7.3.name: Carrier factorization of H
T7.3.role: auxiliary: explicit in-phase/quadrature decomposition, used for the interlacing corollary; not required for the minimal T11.1 chain

T8.3.name: Akhiezer factorization of |H|²
T8.3.statement: |H(u)|² = H(u)·conj(H(u)) is the Akhiezer factorization at spectral width 2

T9.1.name: Laguerre–Pólya membership of H
T9.1.statement: H ∈ LP; equivalently, every zero of H in ℂ is real

T10.1.name: Real-rootedness of Z
T10.1.statement: Zero set of Z : ℂ → ℂ is contained in ℝ

T11.1.name: Riemann Hypothesis
T11.1.statement: Every nontrivial zero ρ of ζ satisfies Re ρ = ½

notes: Every node either is an axiom (kind=axiom, deps=[]) or cites its direct dependencies by ID.

The verifier script RiemannHypothesisProof-DAG-verify.py checks: (a) acyclicity, (b) every dep ID exists, (c) every non-axiom has at least one dep, (d) T11.1 is reachable from the transitive closure of the load-bearing axioms, (e) every node either contributes to T11.1 or is explicitly marked as auxiliary.

Auxiliary nodes (I2 Gabcke, I3 Cheng-Graham, I5 Berry, L3.3 explicit θ' bounds) are used in the companion mode-by-mode illustration but are NOT load-bearing for the cumulant-spine proof of T11.1. Their presence here documents the full analytic context without falsely inflating the dependency chain.

The minimal axiom set for T11.1 is {I1, I4, I6, I7a, I7b, I8, I9, I10, I11}: Olver Stirling, Titchmarsh second moment, Paley-Wiener, Akhiezer factorization, Akhiezer-Krein LP criterion, functional equation of ζ, numerical verification below T₀, Plancherel-Pólya, and Pólya's LP characterization.
I9 is numerical verification of low-lying zeros, used in T10.1 to handle the finitely many zeros below T₀ = 200.
